title: recorded hook returns + determinism ledger
manager: The effect boundary made real — external calls record their answers, and replay consumes the recordings instead of re-firing the world. This is what makes a machine with side effects still replayable, and it is what finally lets receipts cover hooked machines: the v14 scope limit that said "only pure machines can be attested" dies right here. An irreversible, because the recording format is pinned forever.
cs: fills irreversibles #4; per-call record {call-site id, args-hash, return, seq} in a sidecar ledger beside the tape (tape stays stimulus-pure); replay: missing recording = refusal (§15), re-fire only under explicit `--live`; idempotency annotations honored after crash.
